También puedes luchar codo con codo con un amigo.nnLos combates funcionan como la esgrima, con unos cuantos movimientos diferentes, incluida la parada. Los niveles se suceden, con un jefe al final de cada uno. Ganas dinero derrotando a tus enemigos y excavando en busca de tesoros en el lugar marcado con una X.

meta.name: Skweek
meta.desc:
Los Skweek vivieron en armonía durante siglos, siendo un pueblo pacífico por naturaleza, hasta que llegó Pitark. Arruinó su tierra rosa brillante al eliminar el color, y fue solo después de su muerte que tuvieron la oportunidad de limpiar la tierra. Tu trabajo es viajar por los 99 continentes y limpiar cada uno en secuencia.
Skweek puede moverse en cada una de las cuatro direcciones de la brújula y está armado con un arma simple, cuyos disparos rebotan antes de golpear a uno de los secuaces de Pitark, los Schnoreuls, cuyo contacto es instantáneamente fatal. Algunos cuadrados contienen flechas que te obligan en una dirección particular, mientras que también hay bloqueos que debes evitar. Los cuadrados de bomba explotan los cuadrados circundantes pero te dan tiempo para alejarte de ellos mientras lo hacen, mientras que los cuadrados de atajo te permiten moverte a otro cuadrado de tipo teletransportador empujando el joystick en su dirección.

meta.name: SkyChase
meta.desc: Take to the skies and fight your opponent (computer or human) on a split screen or null-modem link.  Takeoffs and landings are handled automatically, so players can focus on shooting each other down. Flyby replays are featured for crashes, so you can gloat over each victory. In one-player mode, the view from your opponent's plane is optional; either way you have a scanner to help locate them. Different levels of ammunition and missile stock can be set, to make for a variety of gameplay options. After the game has been ran for 3 hours, a hidden 'paper plane' mode is added, alongside a roster of topline machines such as the F-16 and MiG-29.

meta.name: Skyfox II : The Cygnus Conflict
meta.desc:
Skyfox II es un juego de combate espacial, con muchas misiones diferentes y gráficos bien detallados para su época. La jugabilidad es rápida, divertida y sorprendentemente variada. Pilotarás un caza estelar a través de diversas misiones, desde localizar artefactos perdidos hasta destruir al enemigo. Las naves enemigas, los cazas Xenomorfo en particular, están muy lejos de las naves ficticias de la mayoría de los primeros juegos de disparos: esquivan tus disparos, se agrupan a tu alrededor y son mortalmente precisos en sus ataques.
Skyfox II también tiene elementos de simulación y estrategia, puedes planear tu ataque, fijar un rumbo en el mapa y utilizar el modo de piloto automático. También tienes que repostar de vez en cuando, lo que naturalmente te coloca en una posición vulnerable, ya que las naves enemigas están siempre presentes.

meta.name: Skyfox
meta.desc: Los alienígenas han invadido tu amada patria y debes ayudar a salvarla. Pilotas el Skyfox, el caza de ataque más avanzado disponible en la Federación. La vista del juego es desde la cabina. Skyfox dispone de escudos deflectores, cañones láser, cinco misiles guiados y cinco misiles buscadores de calor, así como de una velocidad máxima de Mach IV. Tendrás que luchar contra tanques, aviones enemigos y el camión cisterna. Utiliza el radar para localizar vehículos enemigos y el escáner de largo alcance para asegurarte de que ninguna oleada llega a tu base antes de que puedas salir. Vigila tus escudos y niveles de combustible, también puedes atracar en la base para repostar.

meta.name: Sky High Stuntman
meta.desc: Get that radical stunt scene right or get cut! Wipeout with JETS, CHOPPERS, SPACE SHIPS and BI-PLANES. Thrust through WAR SCENES, ACTION MOVIES, COP FILMS and SCI-FI EPICS. Use sophisticated LASER, MISSILE and ROCKET stunt equipment. Mega action! Compulsively addictive.

meta.name: Slackskin & Flint
meta.desc:
Something is tearing apart the fabric of time, vortices are absorbing living creatures end dispersing them at random throughout the history of the world.
Into this chaos ore drawn Slackskin and Flint, dinosaurs from the planet's distant past. They stumble into a vortex and ore sent spinning into another time zone. Frightened and confused, they realize their only way to escape the maelstrom and return home lies in following the vortices to their source, but as all of the other creatures have somehow been turned against them, it will not be easy.
Join Slackskin and Flint on their magical adventure through time in this wonderfully funny and thoroughly entertaining game.
Control both our heroes using the game's simultaneous two player option.

meta.name: Slamtilt
meta.desc: Slam Tilt es un juego de pinball ambientado en un entorno 3D. El juego cuenta con cuatro mesas diferentes, cada una con diferentes diseños y características. Se incluye un gran número de subjuegos, así como una variedad de animaciones en el panel LCD de la parte superior. Cada mesa tiene funciones multibola, y la vista cambia automáticamente a pantalla completa cuando se activa. En total hay seis puntos de vista diferentes, y se prometen nada menos que 47 modos de juego distintos.

meta.name: Slayer
meta.desc: As the ruthless Slayer you have hordes of enemies to wipe out in this horizontally-scrolling shooter. The paths are narrow and treacherous in a similar way to Scramble, so shooting out the wall-mounted installations is important. There are plenty of bonus weapons and shields available however, and 5 lives in total. Each of the 3 levels ends with a boss to defeat.

meta.name: Sleeping Gods Lie
meta.desc: Normally this type of deep and involved adventure game is just my cup of tea, especially if it is in a 3-D world vein. Sleeping Gods Lie though is an odd game. The puzzles are in there just waiting to be found and the game has considerable depth but to its detriment there is a heck of a lot of wandering around vast, barren terrain as well. The slow moving pace put me off initially but once I got down to some serious mapping it became quite a compelling adventure romp. The accompanying sound effects are surprisingly poor but the title screen music has a certain charm. Sprite expansion is well implemented but I found the character animation and movement occasionally a bit messy to look at. In short, a little long-winded but enjoyable all the same.

meta.name: Sleepwalker
meta.desc: Sleepwalker es un juego de plataformas y aventuras para Amiga. Controlas a Ralph, un perro que debe evitar que su amo sonámbulo Lee se despierte. Por tanto, debes hacerle evitar obstáculos, no dejarle caer al agua, hacerle cruzar agujeros, etc... Al final de cada nivel te espera un nivel extra en el que podrás recoger monedas y objetos. Hay seis enormes niveles principales y cinco niveles de bonificación.

meta.name: Sliders
meta.desc:
A simple enough futuristic sports game that isn't a world away from Marble Madness. You control a ball over a futuristic landscape, the idea being to find the puck that's hidden somewhere in the level and "herd" it back to your starting point. Sounds simple enough, except for one thing, there's another player (either computer or human) trying to do the same thing.
This results in incredibly hectic gameplay. Add this to the fact that sometimes you're negatively charged to the puck (and thus kick it away) or positively charged (so the puck sticks to you) and you're in for a crazy time!

meta.name: Slightly Magic
meta.desc:
Slightly Magic is a platformer-adventure game not unlike Codemasters' own Dizzy and Seymour series, but is generally shorter and easier than most other games of that genre.
In Slightly Magic you are cast as Slightly - mischievous nephew of legendary wizard Bigwiz, who has suddenly taken off on some urgent business, leaving his castle in disarray with dragons and magical spells running amok. One of the dragons kidnaps the princess, and now Slightly has to save her.
In addition to standard inventory puzzles of Dizzy games, Slightly Magic introduces a magic system that requires multiple objects to be combined in order to achieve effects ranging from frightening the enemies to turning the player into a fish.

meta.name: SlipStream
meta.desc: Slip Stream is a shoot 'em up with a third person chase view similar to Space Harrier. The player controls an aircraft called the emerald fighter and has to fly through nine levels (here called streams) which have been overtaken by alien forces. In each stream there are various defence structures and crafts that are powered by a crystal located at the end of the stream. It is the player's task to destroy these crystals and thereby liberate the streams. Like in other chase view shooters the player's craft is automatically moving forward while the player can steer it left and right, climb, dive and shoot. Movement is restricted to stream though which is located in the centre of the screen. Various obstacles and enemies will come in the player's path and these have to be either avoided or shot down. Getting hit by a projectile or colliding with a structure will lead to a life lost and the level has to be replayed.

meta.name: Sly Spy Secret Agent
meta.desc: En el papel de un agente secreto inspirado en James Bond, tu misión es detener a toda costa a una organización terrorista llamada Consejo para la Dominación Mundial. Después de recibir el informe, se le lleva por aire al lugar donde comienza la acción. Saltando del avión, caes libremente a la zona de aterrizaje. Varios tipos malos se unen a tu posición e intentan eliminarte. Un aterrizaje exitoso conduce a ocho niveles de advertencia que incluyen escenas como obras de construcción, fábricas, acción submarina y una persecución en moto.

meta.name: Smash T.V.
meta.desc: El año es 1999. El programa de televisión más popular del mundo es Smash TV, un concurso ultraviolento entre dos combatientes armados hasta los dientes, desatado en una serie de escenarios mortíferos que están llenos hasta el estallido de hordas de bestias sin cerebro y robots asesinos. El objetivo del juego es destruir todo a la vista e intentar obtener más efectivo y premios que el otro jugador. Los hornos tostadores, las videograbadoras, los autos sofisticados y las vacaciones de lujo están ahí para tomar... para cualquiera que pueda sobrevivir el tiempo suficiente para disfrutarlos. Smash TV es un increíble shoot-em-up del cerebro de Eugene Jarvis, creador de Robotron: 2084. Mantiene el estilo de juego frenético y el método de control de dos joysticks, pero agrega mucho gore, violencia, un toque de sátira y ese modo de dos jugadores de suma importancia.

meta.name: Snapperazzi
meta.desc: You play the Snapper, a small but willing photographer who wants to get into the world of the paparazzi. To prove his worth to The Sun editor, he has to race around some large, jerky levels, taking pictures of some of the most misshapen sprites ever seen, while collecting rolls of film and trying to avoid bills, shops and anything else that might try to takes his money. He also needs to collect enough delicious Domino pizzas, so he can then build a space ship to take him to the next level.

meta.name: Sneech
meta.desc: Imagine Tron with up to 10 cycles on a screen which keeps filling up with walls and traps, and tons of powerups to collect. Vroom-vroom cycle noises and crazy spot sound effects taken from some weird Salvador Dali alternate universe. Plus a shop with a 3-page list of powerups, its not hard to imagine this game can be fun. Sneech is an excellent party game for up to 6 players. Another good game in the Amiga's strong library of shareware and freeware releases.

meta.name: Snoopy : The Cool Computer Game
meta.desc: Linus ha perdido su sábana favorita. Su mundo se desmorona. Linus intenta esperar mientras Snoopy investiga para encontrar la preciada sábana.

meta.name: Snow Strike
meta.desc:
You must fly an F14-LBC "Rapier" on a series of missions all along Colombia. You've land missions like free flight, destroy drug labs, bomb drug refineries, destroy drug stores and destroy drug convoy. You've aircraft carrier missions like free flight, destroy enemy aircraft carriers, destroy drug ships and destroy enemy oil supplies. 
In the air you can fight the MiG-27, Su-27, MiG-29 and MiG-31 armed with heat or radar guided missiles!
You can choose to be an existing pilot or to create a new one. 
You can choose between 2 levels of difficulty: beginner (less enemy attacks) or expert (the most dangerous).
The weather will affect the mission. The good weather will be great for training, and the stormy will be dangerous but great for attacks, as you can hide into the clouds and approach the enemy unnoticed.
You'll have a copilot that will help you telling what's going on every moment. 
You can avoid missiles using your skills, using chaff or... if you can't, you can use your parachute!

meta.name: Soccer Kid
meta.desc: Poco tiempo antes de la celebración de Mundial de Futbol de 1994, una nave alienígena que se dedica a coleccionar trofeos de diversos planetas quiere hacerse con la preciada copa de campeón del mundo. Siguiendo la costumbre de los extraterrestres, abducen el preciado trofeo pero tienen tan mala suerte que en su viaje espacial un asteroide se cruza en su camino y rompe la copa en cinco que pedazos que caerán en diversas zonas de la Tierra: Inglaterra, Italia, Rusia, Japón y Estados Unidos. Cuando esta noticia llega a oídos de Soccer Kid, este decide ponerse los botines y, armado únicamente con su pelota preferida, realizar un viaje alrededor del mundo para conseguir recomponer el trofeo.

meta.name: Soccer Pinball
meta.desc:
Soccer Pinball is a pinball game with eight round split across three tables. On the soccer-themed levels, a 'goal' is guarded by 'defenders' who must be removed by hitting them. Score three goals to reach the next level - initially you will face another soccer-based table with more defenders. Other football-themed elements include 'throw-in' positions from the side and pop-up players to toggle for points. 
On the Amiga version, the later tables have a casino and gambling as a theme. Gameplay is fundamentally the same - hit the correct targets in sequence to advance, with other targets adding to your score.

meta.name: Soldier 2000
meta.desc: Soldier 2000 is an action game developed and published by Artronic Productions, released in Europe in 1990. Set in a dystopian future, the story revolves around a hostage crisis caused by unknown factions. You control an elite anti-terrorist squad composed of tough, skilled female operatives. Although they are formidable, they can't carry excessive weight; overloaded characters will slow down and struggle to move. Players can choose from 14 weapon options, including handguns, machine guns, grenade launchers, and experimental pulse lasers, as well as essential gear like infrared sights. As you equip your troops, the selected items appear on the left side of the screen. The game begins with a 2D view of the player in a corridor, facing potential attacks from both sides. Players can collect ammo, bullets, and grenades while strategically dropping proximity mines to manage threats.

meta.name: Soldier of Light
meta.desc:
Encarnas a Xain, el Soldado de la Luz, cuya misión consiste en visitar diversos planetas invadidos y eliminar a las hordas malignas que han atacado a las poblaciones humanas. La mayor parte del juego se desarrolla como un shoot 'em up horizontal: controlas a Xain, quien puede saltar o agacharse para esquivar enemigos, y también saltar hasta repisas más elevadas. A lo largo del recorrido puedes recoger una amplia variedad de armamento adicional, aunque pierdes todo el equipamiento extra al perder una de tus cinco vidas.
Desde el inicio, puedes seleccionar libremente los planetas Lagto, Cleemans y Cleemalt —cada uno con su entorno característico: Cleedos es un planeta desértico, Cleemalt cuenta con paisajes cavernosos y Lagto se presenta exuberante y verde. Tras completar cada planeta, debes realizar con éxito un tramo de vuelo para llegar al siguiente destino.

meta.name: Sonic Boom
meta.desc:
Sonic Boom es un videojuego arcade de género shoot 'em up con desplazamiento vertical, en el que encarnas a un piloto estadounidense al mando del avión ultrapotente Sonic Boom. Tu misión consiste en superar seis niveles repletos de enemigos dispuestos como carne de cañón para evitar la destrucción del mundo. Cuentas con hasta 5 continuaciones, cada una otorgando 3 vidas adicionales.
Al final de cada nivel te aguarda un jefe (boss) que deberás derrotar dentro de un límite de tiempo para poder avanzar. La mayoría de los enemigos son aeronaves, aunque también deberás eliminar tanques, trenes e instalaciones de torretas. Tras derrotar oleadas de enemigos, obtendrás armamento mejorado y otros bonos que potenciarán tus capacidades en combate.

meta.name: Sooty & Sweep
meta.desc:
Sooty y Sweep son dos famosos personajes de la televisión británica que llevan más de cincuenta años apareciendo en diversos programas. Este juego de plataformas es el primero protagonizado por estos personajes, que se han metido en un buen lío.
Matthew se ha ido y ha dejado a Sooty a cargo de la casa. El problema es que Sweep ha dejado todos sus viejos huesos por toda la casa y ha dejado la puerta del jardín abierta, permitiendo que entren todo tipo de insectos. El juego consiste simplemente en recoger todos los huesos mientras evitas a las criaturas reptantes que ocupan la casa.

meta.name: Sooty's Fun With Numbers
meta.desc: Six short maths tests for children up to 7 years are combined in this pack. Sooty, the silent magic bear, one of the most famous British children's characters, is accompanied by his friends Sweep, the squeaking dog, and Soo, the talking bear. The games are simple and require little computer knowledge, with full graphics. In "Magic Numbers" the player must count how many magic wands Sooty has on the screen. "Sweep's Bones" is about moving bones. "Soo's Hats" is an addition test. "Bigger/Smaller" requires choosing the larger or smaller number. In "Order Order" missing numbers must be correctly sorted. "Soo's Race" combines these skills in a racing game.

meta.name: Sophelie
meta.desc: Sophelie is a horizontally scrolling shooter where you control a bird. You travel through three parts of the game fighting various enemy types including spinning planets, killer trees and giant eyeballs. Additional lives can drop from dead enemies and the game can be continued if you die. The backgrounds feature colorful parallax scrolling effects that change with each part of the game.

meta.name: Sorcerer Lord
meta.desc:
El ejército del malvado Señor de las Sombras amenaza con invadir la pacífica tierra de Galanor. La alianza de las razas de Galanor se prepara para la guerra.
El movimiento y el combate se basan en turnos. Tú y el Señor de las Sombras reclutáis nuevas tropas alternativamente, movéis ejércitos por un mapa hexagonal e intentáis conquistar guarniciones enemigas. Algunas razas luchan con más fiereza en determinados tipos de terreno. La magia es un elemento aleatorio de poca importancia en las batallas y no está bajo tu control directo.

meta.name: Sorceror's Apprentice
meta.desc:
Actual Screenshots' game, Sorceror's Apprentice, places you in the part of wisened old man blessed with the King's magic powers. The reason for this gilt is because the King wants you to free the land from the forces of darkness.
I must admit to finding the gameplay of Sorceror's Apprentice somewhat tiresome and monotonous albeit playable. The first few credits kept me addicted so that I could progress to allow me to see later levels, but I soon saw it was a case of Ã¢â‚¬Å“seen it, done itÃ¢â‚¬Â. The graphics are far superior to the sound, although I still wasn't satisfied with what should be up to sixteen-bit standard.
In terms of graphics, Sprites are nicely drawn that jump and move fairly smoothly across bland and simplistic backdrops. The explosions are rather poor, and the overall effect is polished but could be hotter. Sounds are slightly worst than the graphics, as there is nothing to write home about.
Overall, a great disappointment that is best avoided.

meta.name: Sorcery+
meta.desc:
La Tierra ha caído en el Vacío Oscuro. El demonio Nigromante y sus demoniacos sirvientes la han conquistado. Esclavizando a todo el mundo. Pero todavía queda un rayo de esperanza: TÚ.
Eres el último de los grandes brujos y debes viajar desde tu tierra a Stonehenge donde están cautivos tus tres compañeros brujos.
El Nigromante ha enviado a sus sirvientes en tu persecución y el tiempo se agota. ¿Puedes salvarlos antes de que hayan sido convertidos en piedra y se hunda la tierra en una oscuridad eterna?
En muchas de las pantallas se encontrarán objetos que puedes utilizar o no. En el juego solamente uno de los objetos destruirá al Nigromante: este debe colocarse en altar de Stonehenge. El objeto erróneo dará por resultado tu muerte.

meta.name: Sorefist
meta.desc: An attempt at creating a clone of the arcade and 8-bit home computer classic Renegade. The game, however, doesn't feel finished: There are only three levels and enemies hardly ever attack and the music has some corrupted samples.

meta.name: Space Ace II : Borf's Revenge
meta.desc: Space Ace II is a essentially a cartoon strip in which our hero will encounter numerous hazards. You have to avoid traps and enemies by pressing the right key at the right time: cursor keys for movements, Insert key to shoot. You never control Dexter directly, you just choose an appropriate direction. Each of the 27 seconds-long scenes requires one to three keystrokes. If your decision is wrong, the cartoon will end with a death animation, and Dexter will lose one of his three lives. So you're basically trying to keep the strip running. Timing is crucial; you will need to press keys in the exact second. Luckily, you're allowed to save your progress at four fixed points.

meta.name: Space Ace
meta.desc:
El héroe intergaláctico Space Ace se enfrenta al malvado Comandante Borf, quien lo golpea con un Infanto-Ray, convirtiendo a Ace en una versión adolescente de sí mismo llamada Dexter. Ahora Ace / Dexter no solo debe rescatar a su novia Kimberly, sino también evitar la destrucción de la Tierra a manos de Borf.
El juego consiste en la película de dibujos animados que se ejecuta por sí sola, y el jugador debe presionar el botón derecho en el momento adecuado (ya sea moviéndose o disparando el arma de Ace) o ver una de las muchas animaciones de muerte.

meta.name: Space Assault
meta.desc: What happened so far... Strangers from the planet Dumcleps sent some ships to attack planet Enacirruh. They destroyed the cities and killed civilians without any reason. Their aim was to occupy the planet. The United Galaxy Forces charged you to rescue the planet and to defeat the evil. There is not much time until the whole planet is ruined. Try your best!

meta.name: Space Crusade : The Voyage Beyond
meta.desc:
ATTEMPT TO GUIDE YOUR SPACE MARINES THROUGH  THE MOST HAZARDOUS AND GRUELLING MISSIONS EVER!
The monsters that await all those who would take up the challenge are the most Fearsome and powerful ever discovered in the known universe. Watch out for the entrails sucking Vilous Parasites and deadly Space Ogres!
Success will bring honour failure is the end of all hope!!

meta.name: Whirligig
meta.desc: WHIRLIGIG - The first-ever game to use LIGHTSOURCE 3D - Maelstrom's revolutionary system of shaded ultra-solid 3D graphics! An epic all-action game featuring lightning-fast speeds of play, graphic techniques BEYOND the arcade, 4 billion sectors to explore, 100 billion enemy ships to blast, and an awesome final objective to reach!

meta.name: Space Harrier - Return To The Fantasy Zone
meta.desc: Run? fly? dodge those obstacles, but return fire on wave after wave of attacking aliens. Armed with a really big gun, you have to defend yourself against the alien hordes using your wits to navigate around the screen. But watch out for those end of level bosses! Originating in the arcades, Space Harrier is a third-person rail shooter with pseudo-3D graphics, set in the same world as Fantasy Zone. The lone titular hero fights aliens with a fireball-launching cannon, which can also be used to transform the hero into a flying rocket. The protagonist is constantly in motion, but the speed of his movement can be regulated by the player.

meta.name: Space Harrier II
meta.desc: Space Harrier II sigue (muy de cerca) los pasos de su predecesor y te involucra a ti (un Space Harrier) para salvar "Fantasy Land". Esto, combinado con los muchos colores llamativos y destellantes que el juego arroja sobre ti. Hay 13 etapas para completar lanzando chorros y bombardeando a los malos y a los jefes a medida que se acercan. El juego se juega detrás del personaje principal y el paisaje se apresura en 3D similar al efecto utilizado en muchos juegos de coches como Outrun. El juego usa muchos de los enemigos y jefes del primer Space Harrier, pero también agrega una serie de nuevos.

meta.name: Space Hulk
meta.desc:
Space Hulk is a real-time conversion of the original board game by Games Workshop set in the Warhammer 40,000 universe.
Players control a squad of Space Marines in an maze allowing 90 degree turns which they may view through multiple first person cameras or using an overhead map. Their mission: to wipe out the Genestealers, a vicious alien race inhabiting the derelict ships.
The 5-man squad (sometimes accompanied by another), encased in Terminator armors and armed with long range and melee weapons, can be given tactical orders (executed simultaneously) via map - with an option to temporarily freeze time (to give player more room for thinking).
The game features 51 missions, including tutorial and original Deathwing campaign. The CD-ROM re-release adds extra 9 missions and more digitized speech (in slightly better quality).

meta.name: Space Pilot 89
meta.desc:
Space Pilot '89 is a remake of Space Pilot and a variant of Time Pilot. The background story involves a pilot who gets mixed up in a time trap and visits different time zones. There he has to shoot a certain amount of enemies to get access to the next level which increases the difficulty. Enemies either try to ram the player or shoot rockets.
New in comparison to the original Space Pilot is the power up system: shot enemies may leave symbols behind which grant advantages when picked up, e.g. a shield, automatic fire (both for a limited amount of time) or another weapon. The symbol changes if it is shot. The game scrolls in multiple directions and all few levels there is a boss to defeat.

meta.name: Spaceport
meta.desc: "Spaceport" is a fast-paced shoot 'em up for the Commodore Amiga that is a successful clone of the C64 classic "Fort Apocalypse". The game consists of two action-packed levels in which the player controls an armed spaceship and must assert himself against enemy forces. The gameplay in "Spaceport" demands quick reflexes and precise controls as the player navigates through tight, labyrinthine environments, shooting enemies and avoiding obstacles. Similar to "Fort Apocalypse", the focus is on exploring the environment, destroying enemy turrets and vehicles, and strategically maneuvering through the levels to complete the missions.

meta.name: Space Quest II : Chapter II - Vohaul's Revenge
meta.desc:
So you thought you'd seen the last of Sludge Vohaul? Think again! Like every evil scientist is supposed to, Vohaul returns with an even meaner scheme. This time he intends to infest the planet Xenon with thousands of genetically engineered door-to-door life insurance salesmen. The last thing he wants is you, ace-janitor Roger Wilco, messing things up and so you are captured and brought to the planet Labion to spend the rest of your days working in Vohaul's mines. Fortunately the shuttle that takes you to the mines crashes and you're the only survivor.
Can you survive the dangers of the jungle planet Labion?nnCan you stop the evil Sludge Vohaul?
Space Quest 2: Vohaul's Revenge is the second game in Sierra's humorous Space Quest series of adventure games. It's a classic adventure game with a text-parser: you move Roger Wilco around with arrow keys, and interact with the world by typing commands. As in every Space Quest game, there are lots of ways to die.

meta.name: Space Quest IV : Roger Wilco and the Time Rippers
meta.desc: Tras sus divertidas aventuras en el juego anterior, Roger Wilco se relaja en su pub favorito en algún lugar del tiempo y el espacio cuando soldados fuertemente armados entran en la habitación. Trayendo un mensaje de despedida de su antiguo némesis, Sludge Vohaul, planean deshacerse del héroe de la limpieza a modo de ejecución. Eso es, hasta que un hombre con un secador de pelo enorme ayuda a Roger a escapar a través de una grieta temporal hacia el futuro. Ahora Roger Wilco ha despertado en su planeta natal, en algún momento de una metaficcional Space Quest XII. Es un futuro sombrío y distópico: la serie se ha ido al traste sin su héroe, y Vohaul reina con supremacía. Roger debe encontrar la manera de evitar a los secuaces de Vohaul, cumplir su destino y descubrir algunas sorpresas que le aguardan en su propio futuro.

meta.name: Space Quest 1 : Roger Wilco In The Sarien Encounte
meta.desc:
Six years after its first appearance, the classic sci-fi send-up is back with a vengeance. Equipped with an all-mouse game system, and shrill Nineties graphics is sets out to enrapture the next generation of players.
Prepare to save the world, well Xenon, our hero's home planet, as Roger Wilco tumbles into The Sarien Encounter. Inconveniently for Roger - he's in the middle of a nap - the universe has chosen this exact moment to begin falling apart. No more sleep for Roger.
Full to the brim with Hitchhiker-type humour, it puts the threadbare fabric of recent adventures to shame. It should be an integral part of every ambitious collection, if you have an A3000. Everyone else will be sorely tired by long loading sessions, slow animation and gritty graphics. Sci-fi fanatics must play it, others still can, though Sierra should do their homework and deliver versions that are as much fun on a standard Amiga because they deserve to remain classics.

meta.name: Space Ranger
meta.desc:
Space Ranger takes the Defender theme, and develops it into a far superior game. A multitude of highly imaginative, friendly looking critters (as they are called), innocently go about their daily business. Most are ground-based, and run up and down or leap into the air. Others swoop around the sky, merrily tweeting and quacking. All this is set against a three speed parallax scrolling background of mountains and forests.
But here come no-good aliens to put a downer on things. A mother ship hovers in wait for any unsuspecting critters to wander beneath it. It then turns on a tractor beam which pulls them into the craft. A well aimed shot will kill these, freeing the poor little cubes. On the other hand, a poorly aimed shower of laser fire could easily result in a critter being shot.
Space Ranger's appeal is mainly in its cuteness. The effective backgrounds make the game a pleasure to watch. Defender variants are nothing new, but this is the best I've seen. Space Ranger is also fun to play, and whilst not stunning, is an example to be followed by other budget companies. Definitely one of the best cheapies around. Highly recommended.

meta.name: Space Rogue
meta.desc: Space Rogue es un juego híbrido que combina una simulación de vuelo espacial y combate con elementos de comercio y rol, como la interacción entre personajes y una estructura basada en misiones con una trama principal. El vuelo espacial tiene lugar en dos modos diferentes: Vuelo de crucero, ideal para viajes rápidos, y vuelo newtoniano, ideal para el combate y el vuelo de precisión. La exploración y el combate en 3D también se llevan a cabo desde distintas vistas de cámara. Cada sistema planetario tiene una variedad de destinos y obstáculos que atravesar.

meta.name: Space Station
meta.desc: Space Station is a platform action adventure in which the player takes the role of a space ship commander. The station is attacked by aliens and must be destroyed. Sadly, the floppy disk with the destruction code is hidden somewhere in the 99 rooms and must be found first. To be able to escape from the station, fuel for a space ship has to be collected during the way. The player can jump and shoot, although before using the weapon, ammunition packs must be found and therefore using the pistol is very limited. In the station robots and aliens are attacking and lifts, teleporters and jump-pads allow the player to navigate through the many different connections between the rooms. The screen flicks between the rooms, no scrolling is involved. There is only one life, but a generous energy level, which is decreased on contact with enemies. No falling damage occurs, but jumping into the void instantly ends the game.

meta.name: Federation Quest 1 : B.S.S. Jane Seymour
meta.desc: In this sci-fi RPG you are sent to a fleet of spaceships which have been invaded by aliens who escaped during a routine mission. This involves walking around and fighting off any aliens you encounter, while collecting keys for the doors. A toxic coolant must be handled carefully if this is to be achieved. 6 programmable robots can help with repair chores The game's control system has a direction pad for moving and inventory mapping.

meta.name: Special Forces
meta.desc:
En tiempos de tensión política, ejércitos secretos como el SAS, Spetznaz y Delta Force están en primera línea de los conflictos internacionales.
Son las Fuerzas Especiales las que han ganado -y perdido- las operaciones militares más espectaculares, tanto en tiempos de paz como de guerra.
En Special Forces, la secuela del aclamado Airborne Ranger, diriges un equipo especialmente entrenado, equipado y organizado para operar de forma encubierta en territorio enemigo.
Puedes entrar en zonas de conflicto en cuatro regiones climáticas diferentes para sabotear, asesinar o rescatar; tendrás que utilizar tácticas de combate cuerpo a cuerpo, así como técnicas individuales de supervivencia, para completar tu misión antes de que te detecte el enemigo.
Solo, oculto y contra todos.

meta.name: Speedball 2 : Brutal Deluxe
meta.desc: Speedball 2: Brutal Deluxe es un videojuego de 1990 basado en un violento deporte ciberpunk con elementos del balonmano y el hockey sobre hielo que recompensa el juego violento además de los goles. El concepto del juego recuerda mucho a la película Rollerball de 1975. El juego original fue desarrollado por Bitmap Brothers, con varios remakes para muchas plataformas desde su publicación. Es una secuela del juego Speedball de 1988.

meta.name: Speedboat Assassins
meta.desc:
You play a real high-flying marine asked to save four famous harbours from enemy forces using just a speedboat and a few missiles.
Viewed from above and behind, like the perspective of a racing game, the boat has to be steered between two lines of mines for the first part of each harbour. Then, with missiles, you must destroy enemy boats and the four communication towers which control them. Later, a helicopter increases your abilities with rocket launchers, speed, maneuverability and smart bombs.
The main sprite isn't bad but the horizon graphics and other sprites are simple and jerky in comparison. At least the water's silly stripes move smoothly. The Amiga version's playable, so you shouldn't have too much trouble reaching the weak shooting sections. This game is not recommended.

meta.name: Buggy Boy
meta.desc:
Buggy Boy is a race game, but not just an ordinary race game. There are five different routes, offroad, north, east, west, south. The offroad course loops around back to the start, the others are long runs with a distinct beginning and ending. You must complete each leg before the time runs out.
Along the way you can pick up all kinds of bonuses. The colored flags are 30 points each, collect all colors in the order as indicated at the top of the screen to get 1000 bonus points. There are several gates the give you 100, 250 or 500 points, and a time gate. Drive through a time gate to collect it, you receive two seconds extra time upon completing a leg. Occasionally there will be a soccer ball on the road, hit it and it will fly away and you get 2000 points.
There are bridges and tunnels, be careful not to fall off the bridge into the water or crash into the tunnel wall. There also are obstacles like boulders, logs and fences. If you hit them it will cost you valuable seconds off your leg time. But if you hit a log or small stone just right, the buggy will ride on two wheels until you hit something again.

meta.name: Spellbound
meta.desc:
The apprentices Sortice and his twin Ceroapp are ready to become certified sorcerers - but just now some nasties had to kidnap their teacher! No teacher means no graduation, and therefore the two have to rescue him by solving eight side-scrolling levels with different themes, e.g. a swamp, a cave system or the Island of Lost Souls. The game is meant to be played cooperatively (including friendly fire), but when playing alone only Sortice is present in the game.
Spell Bound is a platformer with the main goal being to reach the end of the level without being killed by the various enemies. Players have six spells at their disposal which basically boil down to different weapon systems as well as levitation. Each spell except the standard attack drains manna which, exactly like life energy, is refilled by collecting power ups; either lying on the ground or in locked chests which have to be unlocked by finding the correct key first. Other useful objects include crystals for disrupting energy fields, bombs to blow up stone pillars, or a jet pack. Each character can carry one object at a time and, when trying to cast a spell without having the needed manna, will experience negative effects like reversed controls.

meta.name: Dizzy V : Spellbound Dizzy
meta.desc: Spellbound Dizzy es una continuación de las aventuras del huevo y sus amigos, esta vez con más de 100 pantallas con nombres individuales. Dizzy ha quedado atrapado en un mundo extraño mientras experimentaba con un libro de hechizos, y debe encontrar la manera de salir. La jugabilidad es muy similar a la de los juegos anteriores, combinando la resolución de puzles y la manipulación de objetos con saltos de estilo arcade y la evitación de peligros. También hay secciones adicionales de arcade sobre un carro de minas desbocado y algo de buceo.

meta.name: Spellfire the Sorcerer
meta.desc: Spellfire the Sorcerer is an Action Adventure game, developed and published by Codemasters,  which was released in Europe in 1990.

meta.name: Spherical
meta.desc:
Solomon's Key is a clear conceptual influence for this action puzzler. Our hero must guide a ball through a level to the spot marked 'In', by creating and removing blocks to alter its path. The game is prevented from being a pure clone due to the ball, which moves automatically once activated, and can be controlled by using strategically-placed blocks.
Simply reaching the ball usually involves modifying the level layout using these bricks. There are enemies moving on fixed paths which must be avoided, and bonus items to collect. Also watch for bonuses such as invincibility, gravity-alteration and teleporting. You take time to rotate, so the controls aren't truly immediate. The ball takes 20 seconds to start moving, so use this time to plan ahead.

meta.name: Spherical Worlds
meta.desc:
Spherical Worlds, as the name doesn't imply, is a two-dimensional adventure in which you take control of a spherical droid whose mission it is to roam the various sectors of a space station.
On loading the game, you are presented with just two options: Start Game or Enter Teleport Code. The game itself is fairly slow going - your spherical battle droid has to skid about a derelict space station, avoiding holes, sliding over ice, shooting other little robots and things, while searching for the exit to the next level. On level two, it gets harder...
There's not much in Spherical Worlds to get you hooked - yes, you can upgrade your weapons and you get to do all sorts of exciting things like use teleports and collect keys - but the graphics are small and uneventful, as is the gameplay. It's okay in a sub Alien Breed 1 and 2 sort of way, and there are a few marginally distracting moments, but it doesn't hold your attention for long.

meta.name: Spike in Transylvania
meta.desc:
After a rough crossing of the North Sea, Spike the Viking is  washed ashore.  He ends up in Transilvania, which seems to be just an ordinary village.  However, he finds out that his shipmates have been captured, and are being imprisoned in the dungeons of the local castle.  Spike must rescue his friends from the mysterious castle, but first he has to escape from the (apparently friendly) villager's house where he finds himself, as the door won't open.
The game is a mix of puzzles and action.  The action involves you dodging various enemies, such as dogs, ghosts, bats, etc, and avoiding the many traps in the dungeons of the castle.
You only have a limited amount of energy, and if you lose all of it by touching enemies or traps, you lose a life.  If you lose all your lives you lose the game.
The puzzles, which are mostly fairly simple, involve both the use of objects, such as money to bribe a guard, and switches that perform various functions, such as deactivating traps.  
Keys are very important, as they are needed to unlock your shipmates' cells.  Some cells are protected by guards, who must be tempted away somehow to get to the cells they are guarding.

meta.name: Spindizzy Worlds
meta.desc: Spindizzy World: Un complicado y emocionante juego de acción. Supera intrincadas trampas y toneladas de enemigos mientras controlas a GERALD (Gyroscopic Exploration Landing Device). Cerca de 100 niveles, llenos de pendientes pronunciadas y saltos. ¡Completa todos los niveles y conquista todas las estrellas!

meta.name: Spirit of Excalibur
meta.desc:
Oh dear. They certainly do not seem to treat kings like they used to. Take that Arthur, for instance Ã¢â‚¬â€œ ruler of all England, bearer of Excalibur, loved by all... or so you might think. Ã¢â‚¬ËœNot so', say Virgin. In fact, his power base has so tenuous that on his death everything fell apart quite rapidly. Lord Constantine was next in line for the throne and armed with a sword and your wits, you play Lord Constantine on a mission to claim your rightful place at Camelot.
Thankfully avoiding the temptation to re-tell the story of King Arthur yet again, Spirit of Excalibur manages to conjure the same atmosphere, use many of the characters from the legend, yet still be fresh and original Ã¢â‚¬â€œ no mean feat. Splitting Constantine's story into five sections gives realistic goals to achieve, which range from taking the throne and recruiting knights to tracking down Lancelot and finally defeating Morgan Le Fey. At the same time everything is presented very nicely indeed.

meta.name: Spitting Image : The Computer Game
meta.desc:
Spitting Image is based on the long-running British satirical television show with puppet likenesses of political figures and celebrities. A prophet determines that a world war will occur in seven years instigated by a mysterious leader. To avoid this, one ruler of the world must be determined in this beat 'em up involving six rulers: U.S.A.'s Ronald Regan, the U.K.'s Margaret Thatcher, the USSR's Mikhail Gorbachev, South Africa's PW Botha, Iran's Ayathollah Khomeini, and Pope John Paul II.
Having chosen which to play as, you travel to an opponent's country to engage in a best-of-three fight that is judged by Queen Elizabeth II on a postage stamp. You attack each opponent on the ground as well as jumping in the air and each character has a special move like Thatcher's head-butt or Khomeini's beard whip. You can also call for help twice from an aide who will usually throw a projectile at your opponent. If you manage to defeat all 5 opponents, you will meet the mysterious leader in a final showdown in a mud-wrestling pit in the dark where you will only be able to see the eyes of the characters. You can also play 2-player fights, one against another on the same computer.

meta.name: Spot : The Computer Game!
meta.desc: A puzzle game loosely featuring licensing from the 7Up soft drink. The gameplay involves up to 4 players, which can be either human or computer, each taking on spots of a particular colour. Starting from opposite corners, they take it in turns to move, either by 'reproducing' one of their spots to form another one in an adjacent square, or by making a jump, losing the square you did have but taking one two spaces away. When a piece lands next to one of another colour, that piece changes colour into that of their opponent. The winner of each round is either the last player with any pieces left, or the player who has the most pieces left when the level is full.

meta.name: Spy vs Spy : The Island Caper
meta.desc: En continuación del primer juego de Spy vs Spy, ahora nos encontramos a los espías varados en una isla desierta. Esta vez, en lugar de los cuatro elementos que te permiten salir al aeropuerto, los espías deben intentar recoger tres piezas de un misil y escapar de la isla en un submarino antes de que un volcán entre en erupción. Hay algunas trampas que evitar, como arenas movedizas y tiburones. También hay nuevas trampas (hoyos ocultos, napalm...) y el combate cuerpo a cuerpo se realiza con espadas en lugar de palos.

meta.name: Spy vs Spy : Arctic Antics
meta.desc: El Espía Blanco y el mortífero Espía Negro continúan su batalla. Esta vez, el enfrentamiento tiene lugar en el Ártico helado, con el objetivo de encontrar los componentes esenciales de una tecnología totalmente nueva: un cohete totalmente nuevo. Tienes que conseguirlo antes de que lo haga el mortífero espía negro. La temperatura de tu cuerpo corresponde a tu estado de salud. Puedes entrar en calor entrando en un iglú y permaneciendo unos instantes junto al fuego. El tiempo es limitado para completar tu misión, ¡así que no te demores!

meta.name: Spy vs Spy
meta.desc:
El cómic homónimo de la revista cobra vida en este juego diseñado para uno o dos jugadores. La innovadora pantalla dividida muestra a los dos espías al mismo tiempo. Juega con un amigo o contra la máquina.
El Cuervo Negro y el Cuervo Blanco están listos para luchar antes de que suene la campana. Encuentra los artículos necesarios buscando en los cajones, armarios y muebles de la casa. Burla a tu oponente colocando trampas creativas en diferentes habitaciones (una bomba en un cajón de la cómoda, por ejemplo).
La lucha se produce cuando ambos espías entran en la misma habitación. Encuentra armas ocultas en las habitaciones para ayudarte en estos combates, ya que uno de vosotros morirá si os cruzáis.

meta.name: The Spy Who Loved Me
meta.desc: You are James Bond and with your sidekick Agent XXX who's name is Anya Amasova must stop Karl Stromberg. You must use your skills to end Karl Stromberg's goal of world domination and start a war between east and west. Gameplay-wise it is similar to Live and Let Die except you control your famous Lotus Espirit that can turn into things like a boat and submarine. Your Lotus Espirit is equipped with the usual gadgetry such as a Laser, Missiles, and many other things.

meta.name: Stack Up
meta.desc: Un buen título alternativo para este sería Filas, no del tipo que tiene cuando su esposa encuentra lápiz labial en el cuello de su camisa, sino como un equivalente de Columnas. El juego es esencialmente el mismo: grupos de 3 bloques de colores variables caen hacia el fondo de una cuadrícula de 6x18, y debes organizarlos en grupos de 3 del mismo color, excepto que los bloques se agrupan horizontalmente en lugar de verticalmente.

meta.name: Starbirds
meta.desc:
A horizontally-scrolling shoot 'em up from the last days of the basic 68000-powered Amiga. The levels are taller than the screen, so you can move up or down them and take on a selection of the different enemy waves each time.
At the start of the game you choose your primary and secondary weapon. Pressing fire briefly activates the primary; holding it down activates the secondary - therefore you can't use both at the same time. These can be powered up to various levels, and changed around by picking up tokens, although switching weapon or losing a life reduces your firepower initially. Continues and extra lives are on offer, as are a simultaneous 2-player mode, choice of sounds effects and/or music, and a music test menu.

meta.name: Starblade
meta.desc:
StarBlade is an epic space-opera, set in the vastness of the Orion galazy, the year 3001.
The human race has been decimated to a series of small hi-tech colonies by the ruthless, insectile Cephalhydras.
To finally wipe out humanity, Cephalhydras geneticist have produced Genolyn, a hideously mutated Queen brood-mother, who will spawn a legion of super warriors...
Your mission, to board "StarBlade", the most technically advanced starcruiser in the galaxy and to find and eliminate Genolyn before she starts to breed...

meta.name: Starblaze
meta.desc: To prove yourself as a Tamsinian warrior in a brutal intergalactic war, you must overcome numerous waves of hostile aliens and survive to continue the legend. Only quick reactions and lightning-fast action will get you to your goal! StarBlaze is a three-dimensional action game developed by the team behind the 16-bit version of ELITE, Mr Micro. The game combines classic spaceship action with innovative elements: over 100 different attack scenarios, impressive 3D graphics, five colorful backgrounds, as well as power-ups such as shields, smart bombs and enhanced weapons.

meta.name: Star Command
meta.desc: El juego está ambientado en un futuro lejano. La Tierra es un recuerdo lejano, ya que fue destruida hace mucho tiempo. La escasa población humana se concentra en una pequeña parte de la galaxia, llamada "El Triángulo" por su forma, y se ve amenazada por sus vecinos: la frontera Alfa está infestada de piratas, recientemente unidos en torno a un tal "Blackbeard"; la frontera Beta sufre frecuentes emboscadas de una raza alienígena inteligente parecida a un insecto que, según se cree, planea una guerra total. La tercera frontera conduce a un territorio en gran parte desconocido, habitado por robots espaciales cuyo origen no está claro. La única esperanza de la humanidad es el "Star Command", el cuartel general militar. Tú controlas a la tripulación de una nave espacial e intentas garantizar la paz llevando a cabo las misiones que te encomiende el "Star Command".

meta.name: Star Control
meta.desc:
Star Control es un juego de guerra de ciencia ficción que enfrenta a las fuerzas de la Alianza de Estrellas Libres contra las de la depredadora Jerarquía Ur-Quan. Los juegos están diseñados para que usted pueda fácilmente introducirse en el juego, familiarizándose con los menús, las opciones y los controles de los jugadores.
La Alianza y la Jerarquía poseen diferentes tipos de buques de guerra. Cada barco tiene sus propias características de maniobra y de tiro, además de un poder especial único que puede emplear cuando las circunstancias lo dicten.
El juego tiene dos módulos: el Cuerpo a cuerpo, en el que simplemente puede volar la computadora u otro jugador a través de una de las 16 naves diferentes, ya sea en la Jerarquía o las flotas de la Alianza; o, si desea un juego más desafiante, le permite jugar uno de los 9 escenarios preestablecidos.

meta.name: Star Crusader
meta.desc:
Star Crusader is a 3D space combat simulator in which the player takes the role of Roman Alexandria who is in the middle of a galaxy-wide war between the authoritarian Gorenes and rebels.
During the course of the game, the player can decide which of the opposing forces to join. Both sides in the game feature very different crafts. The Gorenes has rather conventional crafts for solidly armed and armored crafts. The rebel side features some unusual weapons like disabler beam, cloaking device, and other special abilities.

meta.name: Stardust
meta.desc:
El profesor Schaumund, el profesor más malvado y retorcido que pueda haber está a punto de esclavizar la galaxia y tomar posesión de la princesa Vol Levi.
Su cobarde plan implica lanzar una avalancha de meteoros en el espacio, con la intención de destruir cualquier resistencia a sus buques de guerra.
Pero aún no está todo perdido. Tienes la nave de combate y el coraje para detener este caos y destrucción, salvar a la princesa y convertir hasta el último meteorito en polvo espacial (STAR DUST).
Así que ponte a prueba y prepárate para el viaje de miedo de tu vida.

meta.name: StarFlight
meta.desc: Starflight is a space exploration game with role-playing elements. The game is completely non-linear, allowing the player to venture to any star system and explore any of the vast amount of available planets. The main plot is advanced by following cryptic clues either received as transmissions at the initial spaceport, or obtained as a result of successful communication with various alien races populating the universe.

meta.name: Starglider
meta.desc:
The Planet Novenia has been attacked by the Egrons, who snuck in by disguising their craft as the protected Starglider species, which Novenia's automated defenses were programmed not to shoot. 
Your task is to fight them off using only an obsolete Airborne Ground Attack Vehicle. This was designed as the ultimate combat machine, with sophisticated hardware up to Draziw Yarg's high standards. It has a fully-rechargeable shield, plasma drive unit, an inbuilt scanner and 10,000-sector map, a top speed of 2550 Urads and two homing missiles.
The game is viewed from within AGAV's cockpit, with control over speed, weapon targeting and firing. There are several control options to decide exactly how the craft works, with fixed or floating sighting, and the option of automatic centering, allowing you to balance the game between easy sighting and advanced shooting ranges.
Repair depots must be visited frequently, as they can refill the weapon bays as well as repairing the ship. Crucially, the Egron Stargliders need 3 missiles to shoot down, so at least 1 visit to a depot is required to complete this job, at which point you move up to the next, harder, level.

meta.name: Star Goose!
meta.desc: Star Goose es un juego de disparos espacial de vista superior en el que te mueves a través de un entorno semi-3D disparando ráfagas y misiles a tus enemigos. Ocasionalmente, el jugador puede entrar en un túnel y recolectar mejoras (munición, escudo o combustible) escalando las paredes del túnel para chocar con los potenciadores.

meta.name: Starlord
meta.desc:
Starlord is a game of exploration and conquest from Mike Singleton, the author of the similarly-focused Midwinter and The Lords of Midnight. As the title implies, Starlord is set in a science-fiction world, as opposed to the fantasy universes of those previous games.
Your family are Starlords of a single planet, but seek dominance of a vast space consisting of 1000 individually-ruled planets. If you are to come out on top you will have to manage resources across each of your starbases, using revenue to ensure enough fuel, food and minerals are available. Trade with other leaders and form tactical agreements.
To actually take over planets, you will have to lead your ships into space combat, which is played out in multiple-view 3D.

meta.name: Star Trash
meta.desc: In StarTrash, the player controls a ball which needs to climb big mountains which are shown in an isometric perspective. The goal is to collect objects which were misplaced in the levels. Jumping in a hole or touching one of the critters causes the loss of a life. Bonus points are gained by collecting objects like money which are spread over the levels.

meta.name: Star Trek : 25th Anniversary
meta.desc: Captain Kirk and the USS Enterprise are representatives of the United Federation of Planets. Their role in space is that of explorers as well as a military presence. The Enterprise routinely encounters strange adventures and bizarre situations, each laid out as a separate "episode" which must be played in order. The first episode involves the USS Enterprise being called to a world to investigate strange "demons" have appeared from the mines and begun attacking the settlers.

meta.name: Starush
meta.desc: In Starush, a horizontally scrolling shooter, the player has to save four worlds based on the four elements (three levels each) from alien attacks. To do so, the player flies from left to right while shooting everything in sight followed by a boss. Then one speciality of the game kicks in: the player flips direction and has to beat the same level again backwards with a new set of enemies and another boss. Collected extra symbols reward upgrades for the weapon and mostly for the means of transportation. The latter also serves as health bar: after each hit an upgrade level is lost. In turn there is only one life.

meta.name: Star Wars
meta.desc:
Star Wars is a conversion of Atari Games' first-person shoot 'em up based around the original Star Wars film.
You take on the role of Luke Skywalker, aiming to destroy the Death Star - which, as any fan knows, involves attacking the 'weak spot' near the exhaust. To even get to this you have to pass swarms of TIE Fighters. Complete the game and it loops back around at ever-increasing difficulty. The game uses vector graphics, which allow lots of action at high speed on comparatively slow systems.

meta.name: Steel
meta.desc: The space ship Steel is so advanced that it is completely operated by robots. As usually in video games, these robots went havoc and have to be put out of business. This job falls to the player who goes on the ship to shut down the security system. He is disguised as robot, but this does not stop the other ones from attacking. So the player wanders around the maze-like ship, consisting of non-scrolling screens, with the ultimate goal to collect eight cartridges. The player can hold up to three at the same time and needs to bring them to the main control panel. The enemy robots can be blasted away with the laser weapon, but contact costs energy. Losing all energy results in death, but it can be refilled in special stations which are spread over the ship. Using them costs points from the high score. There are also some terminals which need to be cleared by solving a mini game. It basically consists of shooting a wall enough within a time limit without being hit by bullets.

meta.name: Steel Empire
meta.desc: Predecessor to the highly succesful (and more complex) Fantasy Empires. A tactical and strategic turn-based game of robotic combat, land conquest, and city siege.

meta.name: Steg the Slug
meta.desc:
Steg the slug has to feed his family of "T'yungunz".  To do this he must blow bubbles to catch the grubs that they feed on and guide them through the level by blowing on them or nudging them past any obstacles in the way to the nest where T'yungunz live.  To complete a level, you must make sure that a certain number of T'yungunz are fully fed so they go to sleep.  If they don't get enough food then eventually they die.
The levels are full of contraptions such as bellows, and obstacles such as spikes and fire that will burst the bubbles.  Some of these can be activated or deactivated using various switches.
Usually Steg navigates each level by walking on the floor, and also he can climb up the walls and even walk on the ceiling.  There are also devices he can contruct out of the parts from the Acme Bionic Bitz Catalogue, such as the Rocket Pack, and Bionic Legs, which allow different ways of getting around each level.

meta.name: Steigar
meta.desc:
You are Steigar and you used to make your living as a mercenary being paid to complete missions big or small as long as the price was right. That was until the CIA managed to find you and take you prisoner, but you were too good at your job and so they have made you join the US Navy and become a helicopter pilot to participate in one man missions against various threats to the USA.
The game is a scrolling horizontal shoot-em-up viewed from the side as you start out on an aircraft carrier and fly over various assigned missions while shooting and killing many waves of enemies including helicopters, planes, boats and tanks that come at you as you move from the left to the right. Complete the mission and it's onto the next one but touch an enemy or its weapon and you lose one of five lives. You have a standard cannon but power-ups can be collected from shooting certain enemies. Also to help you are limited smart-bombs that kill all on the screen.

meta.name: Stellar 7
meta.desc: In Stellar 7, you drive the Raven, the most advanced Terran fighting vehicle. You are up against Gir Draxon and his evil Arcturan army. You will go from star system to star system, fighting his forces. When the enemies have been destroyed, a mothership flies overhead and beams down the Guardian of the star system, a giant enemy ship. When you reach the Arcturus star system, you will fight Gir Draxon!

meta.name: Stone Age
meta.desc: Stone Age is a challenging puzzle game where you are in control of a dinosaur and you have to make him reach the exit of each cave. The dinosaur can only walk on certain blocks on the level: some are just ordinary blocks, some are movable in the direction the arrow points, some crumble after you walk on them, and some have coloured keys which are needed to get past locks of the same colour.

meta.name: Stormball
meta.desc:
Futuristic sports game roughly based on Pong. The idea is to hit the ball into the other half of the area (hopefully going over some point scoring blocks on the way) and hope that it bounces back off the far wall back into your hands (and so you can take another shot). That's of course while your opposition tries to catch the ball for themselves and score some points with it.
You have to pay to play someone and so you "gamble" your entry fee: Lose and you lose your money, win the match and you win more money. Eventually you have enough to challenge the sport's star players and win some big bucks!

meta.name: Stormlord
meta.desc:
La reina Badh ha ejercido una terrible venganza sobre la tierra secuestrando a las hadas. Para restablecer la situación, debes salvarlas.
A medida que avances por los niveles del juego, te encontrarás con hadas a las que podrás salvar tocándolas. Entre los enemigos se incluyen trampas para moscas Venus, lluvia ácida y dragones, que se pueden evitar o matar en las secciones de bonificación. Para alcanzar ciertos niveles, necesitarás la ayuda del águila Mael Dvin. Después de cada nivel, hay un juego de bonificación en el que tienes que recoger lágrimas para ganar puntos.

meta.name: Storm Master
meta.desc:
Storm Master is a 1991 video game for the Amiga, Atari ST and PC, produced by the now-defunct French developer Silmarils, which placed the player as the head of government for a fictional semi-medieval country.
Similarly to most of the games produced by Silmarils, Storm Master took place on a European-fantasy world. Gameplay revolved around the governance and management of the country of Eolia in an ongoing war against its neighbour, Shaarkania, with the ultimate goal of sacking (and, by inference, destroying) all enemy cities.[1][2]
The game was notable for a blend of turn-based strategy interspersed with live-action battles. This took the form of aerial battles against enemy air fleets or air defences and required the player to launch ballistic missiles against enemy ships or batteries. Other real-time elements of the game involved the player conducting a religious ceremony with the goal of increasing the natural wind energy of the nation. This element of gameplay was further enhanced by requiring the player to dispatch government ministers around the country to develop or industrialise various aspects of the state. The game was further noted by Amiga Action as having an innovative 'advance through time' feature, allowing the player some control over the rate at which time passed in the game.

meta.name: Stratego
meta.desc:
The board game Stratego is a more elaborate version of Capture the Flag. Each player starts with 40 pieces; made up by the flag, 5 bombs, and an assortment of 9 types of military units. 
After these have been placed, each player takes it in turn to move their pieces. Other than scouts (which are the least powerful) the units can only move one space at a time.The flag and bombs can't be moved during the game, so watch out for any squares the computer does not move when it otherwise logically should, and these could be your goal.
When you choose a square occupied by an opposition piece, combat arises, and the higher-numbered piece is lost from the game. You have no way of knowing what strength the opposition pieces are until you have hit them. 
This computerized interpretation has 3 different board layouts, a selection of automatic setups with different strategic benefits, and a demo mode.

meta.name: Street Gang
meta.desc: You play as Mickey, a country boy who moves to New York and aims to join a street gang. To prove his worth, he must complete a challenge: cut off a curl of hair from the gang leader. As Mickey walks through the streets, he must fend off waves of enemies in a classic brawler style. He starts by using hand-to-hand combat, but later gains access to weapons like guns, baseball bats, and teargas. Enemies, some of whom hide in garbage bins, drain Mickey's energy, and losing all of it results in losing a life.

meta.name: Street Racer
meta.desc:
Street Racer is a go-kart racing game which combines racing with violence.
Characters can strike opponents with their fists and must avoid explosives littered around the track. The game's characters possess unique power-ups such as the Screaming Banshee, Batmobile, magic carpet and tri-plane.
The game features the ability to race various championships beginning with the Bronze Championship and moving on to progressively more difficult competitions. An alternative one-on-one mode is also available. The game awards points for final race positions, with bonus points given for accolades such as fastest lap. The competitor with the most points after all races wins the Championship. The game includes a "Rumble" mode in which players attempt to force opponents from an arena. The "Soccer" mode is a free-for-all football match with one goal and in which players tackle by colliding with the opponent.

meta.name: Street Rod
meta.desc: Year is 1963 and you're one of the hotshots aspiring to be king of the county. You're given a small sum of money to buy your first car. Tune it for maximum performance and off you go. In the diner where other hotshots are waiting to take your prized possession, you race for money and pink slips and if you win, you get to take home their cars!

meta.name: Street Sports Basketball
meta.desc:
Taking basketball out of poshly-decorated arenas with hotdog sellers, cheerleaders and pre-game entertainments, Epyx presented basketball played as it is by kids all over America. Four different courts are provided - the schoolyard, the alleyway, the parking lot and the backyard. Each of these has its own hazards to play around, such as oil slicks and potholes. The standard basketball dunks, passes and steals are on offer. An opposition player drilling the ball into you sees you gain possession.
One or two players can join in, choosing their team of 3 from the 10 kids you are offered, each with different abilities. There are 3 computer difficulty levels.

meta.name: Strider II
meta.desc:
Un Strider (the warrior) ha sido llamado para salvar a los habitantes del planeta Magenta. Armado con un láser giroscópico y un convertidor de materia, debe abrirse paso a través de un bosque, una ciudad en ruinas, asentamientos subterráneos, los tejados de un edificio y, finalmente, la nave prisión en la que se encuentra el líder de los Megantans.
La jugabilidad es similar a la del original, consistente en correr, saltar y atacar, con un personaje central muy dinámico. La pistola láser puede usarse estando quieto. Los ataques en movimiento con la espada siguen siendo posibles, combinándose ambas armas de forma similar a los juegos de Switchblade. Se pueden recoger fichas que te convertirán en un robot para enfrentarte a los jefes de final de nivel (si recoges las suficientes).

meta.name: Strider
meta.desc:
Juegas como Hiryu, miembro de la hermandad de los Striders, una especie de orden caballeresca moderna, que lucha contra el siniestro "Grand Master Meio", que desea conquistar el mundo. Diezmada su hermandad, usted es uno de los únicos Striders que siguen vivos para buscar venganza. El juego adopta la forma de un juego de plataformas. Armado con tu sable láser (Cypher), tendrás que eliminar a numerosos enemigos en varios niveles que van desde la Unión Soviética hasta la selva tropical.  También puedes colgarte de las paredes con un gancho metálico.
Ocasionalmente, las bonificaciones obtenidas al destruir cápsulas te permitirán aumentar el alcance de tu espada o ir acompañado de un pequeño robot que destruirá a tus enemigos. Apreciamos los espectaculares movimientos del personaje desenvainando su arma o girando en el aire mientras salta.

meta.name: Fighter Bomber
meta.desc:
In the game, the player participates in the annual Strategic Air Command Bombing and Navigation Competition at Ellsworth Air Force Base in South Dakota. Aircrews from around the world come here to compete against each other, undertaking three missions which are monitored and points are awarded. The best aircrew then receives the Curtis E. LeMay Trophy.
The player can choose between six different planes. After having selected the aircraft the player has to select a mission, and then appropriate weaponry for the selected mission from an array of weapons consisting of different types of missiles and bombs.

meta.name: Striker
meta.desc: The first game in the long-running soccer series, which stood out from the crowd with a unique forced perspective 3D view rather than a simple overhead one. The players are drawn large and there is a large scanner to assist with long passing. However, the standard mode has the ball sticking closely to the feet, which makes mazy runs upfield a more useful strategy. There are 64 international teams on offer, with a European-style knock-out cup competition included. Many different formations and strategies are offered.

meta.name: Strip Poker II+ Data Disk 1
meta.desc: What can I say that isn't already implied by the title? This is a computer version of five-card draw poker featuring photos of women who wear fewer clothes the better you do, all rendered in digitized graphics. The basic game includes two models, Suzi and Melissa, but expansion disks were also available.

meta.name: Deluxe Strip Poker
meta.desc: What can be said that isn't already implied by the title? This is a computer version of five-card draw poker featuring photos of women who wear fewer clothes the better you do, all rendered in digitized graphics. The basic game includes two models, Suzi and Melissa, but expansion disks were also available.

meta.name: Strip Poker : A Sizzling Game of Chance
meta.desc: You are a male player, who wants to undress your woman opponent playing cards. In 5-card poker, you bet own money for a round and if you are lucky, then collect winnings and watch your opponent undress. Your opponent removes clothing every time she loses one hundred dollars. You must make her strip five times to win. The master disk contains two opponents, Suzi and Melissa, but you may acquire Data disks to add other opponents like Marlena, Candi, etc.

meta.name: Strip Pot
meta.desc: An erotic variant of the classic Fruit Machine game. Instead of playing for money, winners are treated to full motion video clips of "five of Europe's most beautiful girls" (as stated on the back of the game's box) disrobing for the camera. The player's goal is to successfully strip all five of the models without running out of credits. The more you win, the more they take off.

meta.name: Stryx
meta.desc: You star as half-robot operative Stryx. Dome City is under attack by KILLER CYBORG ASSASSINS! You navigate Stryx through numerous levels on foot, hovercrafts and some weird round eyeball-type flying craft. It's basic side scrolling action/arcade whackiness. This game was released for IBM PC, Amiga and Atari ST systems.

meta.name: S.T.U.N. Runner
meta.desc:
It's 2492 and you must pilot your Sub-Terranean Underground Network (S.T.U.N.) craft at speeds over 900 miles per hour through a series of courses to retain your title as S.T.U.N. Runner, completing each course before time runs out.
There are also enemies on each course, such as cycles, indestructible armored drones, and flying bird-like craft. Shoot or avoid them. If you hit them, you lose one of your shields (your craft has six total) and are slowed down temporarily. The more shields you have at the finish line, the more bonus points you get. Getting hit with no shields does not kill you, however. There are no lives in this game. The only way to lose is to fail to cross the finish line.

meta.name: Stunt Car Racer
meta.desc:
Compite contra un único oponente por el primer puesto en un circuito único, ¡a 30 metros de altura! Empiezas en la cuarta división, , en cada división hay 3 rivales y tendrás que competir contra ellos dos veces. Debes quedar primero para subir de división. La acción se ve desde dentro del coche durante las carreras de 3 vueltas, con la distancia de tu rival siempre viéndose en la pantalla.
Las pistas están llenas de saltos y puentes levadizos. Tu vehículo tiene la capacidad de quemar una cantidad limitada de combustible turbo como una manera de acelerar más rápido y alcanzar velocidades más altas. No dañes el motor demasiado, pués los efectos se mostrarán durante la próxima carrera.
Hagas lo que hagas, no caigas fuera de la pista, ya que esto te costará perder tiempo hasta que colocan el coche de nuevo.

meta.name: Sub Battle Simulator
meta.desc: Sub Battle Simulator is a Simulation game, developed by Digital Illusions and published by Epyx,  which was released in Europe in 1988.

meta.name: Subbuteo
meta.desc:
Subbuteo recreates the famous table football game, which makes for something very different from Sensible/Kick Off/FIFA style games. It's effectively a turn-based strategy game of sorts, as each player has 30 seconds to make their move (within a 30 minute match length), which involves first selecting a player, then aiming the direction in which he will be flicked, then the power and swerve of the flick are determined by the length of time the button is held down for. 
With any luck, your player will hit the ball, and hit it such that it doesn't leave the playing area, and doesn't hit an opponent. If any of those rules are violated, possession passes to the opposition. Before each flick, the team not in possession can make a 'defensive flick' involving moving one of their players into a potential blocking position (without hitting the ball or an opponent, as this is a foul).

meta.name: Subtrade - Return to Irata
meta.desc:
One of the best M.U.L.E. clones ever made, Subtrade: Return to Irata transfers the galactic capitalism of Dani Bunten's timeless classic to an undersea economy.
Despite new species, background graphics, and different commodities, gameplay is still M.U.L.E. at its heart: up to four players vie in a cutthroat competition to be the richest businessman in a futuristic economy. The different phases of M.U.L.E., e.g. land grant, production, and marketing, are preserved intact and are greatly enhanced by intuitive mouse-based interface and 16-bit graphics. The auction interface, which is the best part of M.U.L.E., is still as furious and fun as ever, with computer players that are aggressive enough to pose a real challenge. Unlike Linel's Traders, another M.U.L.E. clone, Subtrade does not introduce new gameplay options or the number of commodities.
Overall, Subtrade is definitely one of the best M.U.L.E. clones, if not THE best. M.U.L.E. fans will definitely enjoy this one, although the disappointing lack of tournament mode means that an average game with 4 players can be finished in less than 1-2 hours.

meta.name: Suburban Commando
meta.desc: Hulk Hogan alias intergalactic hero Shep Ramsey travels through space, crash-lands on earth and beats up the bad guys. The first level is a R-Type-like horizontal space shooter; destroy formations of drones and collect weapon upgrades. After that, you'll enter three platform levels, in which you have to find nine items each. Avoid being shot and dispatch the enemies with kicks and jumps. After each mission, you'll encounter the level boss, who you're supposed to beat up in a Street Fighter clone.

meta.name: Subwar 2050
meta.desc: The year is 2050. Ocean is the next frontier, and war inevitably reached there. As an independent mercenary working for a corporate conglomerate, you are to pilot the latest fighter subs to protect the corporate interests, and rewarded with cold hard cash. With 5 different theater of operations (of varying difficulty) and an interesting mix of aerial and submarine tactics, this game can be described as Strike Commander under the sea, without the narrative.

meta.name: Summer Camp
meta.desc:
In Summer Camp, you play Maximus Mouse. The flag of Wottadump has gone missing, 24 hours before the start of the season. Maximus must find a replacement, and decides to get the one on the moon.
The game is a platform game, made up of four levels, each made up of a collection of inter-connected screens. There are a variety of objects to collect, but the aim on each level is to collect the parts (found in ACME crates) of a vehicle to get to the next one.
When all the parts are in place, there is a sub-game where the vehicle is constructed, although this is only for bonus points.
meta.genre: Plataformas / Corre y Salta con desplazamiento

meta.name: Summer Olympiad
meta.desc: Summer Challenge is a Sports game, developed by Tynesoft and published by Thunder Mountain,  which was released in 1988.

meta.name: Summer Games II
meta.desc: Eight Olympic events feature in this game, and any selection of these can be played at a time. There is no pure sprinting, and only the rowing and cycling can be thought of as pure joystick-waggling. The triple jump involves pressing right to start the hop (before reaching the white line), right again for the step as you land, left as you land for the jump, and then up to propel you through the sand. I high jump, after selecting a height you feel you can clear, you use up and down to control the speed, fire to jump, and then forward to propel you over the bar.

meta.name: Summer Games
meta.desc: Summer Games allows up to eight players to compete in a series of summer themed Olympic events. The gameplay is similar to the other entries in Epyx "games" series. Each player can choose a country to represent, and then takes turns competing in various events to see who will win the gold medal! The game allows you to compete in all of the events sequentially, choose a few events, choose just one event, or practice an event.

meta.name: Summer Olympiad
meta.desc: Summer Challenge is a Sports game, developed by Tynesoft and published by Thunder Mountain,  which was released in 1988.

meta.name: Supaplex
